interactions.summary: Jiang moves from Inferno to Purgatory and uses Cato to contradict Virgil's limbo rule: Cato has left limbo by merit, controls Purgatory, and rejects Virgil's attempt to manipulate him through Marcia.
He introduces the root of Virgil's unreliability as a theory of love: Virgil treats beauty as an image the soul fantasizes about, controls, and tries to possess.
interactions.speaker_attribution: Jiang interpreting Virgil's account of love.

speaker_notes.note: The packet interleaves Jiang's analysis with translated lines and paraphrases from Purgatory.

claims.claim: Cato's presence as guardian of Purgatory proves Virgil wrong because Cato has left limbo even though Virgil claimed merit cannot move a soul from limbo to heaven.
Virgil attempts to gain Cato's permission by invoking his authority in hell and promising kindness to Marcia, which Jiang reads as an attempted bribe.
claims.temporal_scope: Interpretive claim about Virgil's tactic before Cato.

claims.claim: Cato's refusal shows that Virgil has no authority over him and that Virgil misunderstands the moral order of Purgatory.
Jiang identifies Virgil's conception of love as the deep problem: Virgil condemns Dido while Dante saves Beatrice because they hold opposing models of love.
Virgil's love model begins with beauty provoking animal response, then imagination turns the beloved into a fantasy that can be controlled and possessed.
claims.temporal_scope: Jiang's reading of Virgil's theory of love.

signature_moments.moment: Cato becomes the living contradiction to Virgil's map of the afterlife.
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It turns a new character's location into proof that the trusted guide cannot be trusted.

signature_moments.moment: Cato refuses to be moved by the wife Virgil invokes, because freedom has changed what love can demand.
signature_moments.source_phrase: she has no power to move me any longer
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It exposes the gap between Virgil's relational leverage and Purgatory's moral independence.

signature_moments.moment: Virgilian love turns a real person into a fantasy object.
signature_moments.source_phrase: turn this woman into a fantasy woman that you can now control
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It is the sharpest formulation of the love-as-possession model Dante must defeat.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: Roman patriarch and guardian of Purgatory used by Jiang as evidence that Virgil's limbo rule is false.
A model of love as beauty-triggered desire, imagination, control, and possession.
